title: glycosmis puberula var. craibii
intro: Glycosmis puberula var. craibii, also known as Craib's Glycosmis or Thai Glycosmis, is a plant species in the Rutaceae family, native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Thailand and Myanmar. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Glycosmis puberula var. craibii include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce inflammation. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, where it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
